content: The Party told you to reject the evidence of your eyes and ears. It was their final, most essential command. 1984

I really don't understand who goes to a peaceful protest with a gun and trys shooting a ice agents then it's the ice agents fault for defending himself. We are definitely living in 2 different worlds were everybody sees and hears different. R.I.P ITS AWFUL STORY but you just don't do that .

BREAKING: The man brutally murdered by ICE in Minneapolis today FOR NO REASON has been identified as 37-year-old Minneapolis resident and American CITIZEN Alex Jeffrey Pretti.
Alex was a registered nurse with NO CRIMINAL RECORD. He was an athlete and avid outdoorsman. He was a brother and a son. He worked for the VA, where he cared for veterans' health. He had a legal permit to carry, and video clearly shows that he did NOT brandish his gun at ICE officers. He was an ICE observer who was legally filming the ICE agents' actions when he was stopped, detained, DISARMED, and THEN shot several times by ICE agents.
Alex had a permit to carry.
He was legally exercising his 2nd Amendment right, and was legally filming the ICE agents.
He did NOT draw his weapon, which was holstered.
He was ONLY filming when they approached him, and was ONLY holding his camera.
ICE agents had taken his weapon from its holster
BEFORE they shot him.
He was being pinned down by ICE agents when they shot him FOR NO REASON.
It was MURDER.
Let all that sink in.
Rest in power, Alex.
Please share to spread the word!

Videos verified by The New York Times show the man federal agents shot and killed in Minneapolis on Saturday was holding a phone, not a gun, before the agents took him to the ground and shot him. In total, at least 10 shots appear to have been fired within five seconds.
The videos appear to contradict the Department of Homeland Security's account of the fatal shooting of Alex Jeffrey Pretti, 37. The agency said a man "approached US Border Patrol officers with a 9 mm semi-automatic handgun" and they tried to disarm him. The statement did not specify whether the gun was in the man's hands or merely on his body.
In Minnesota, citizens with firearm permits can openly carry handguns. Pretti had a legal permit to carry a firearm, local police said.
SECOND AMENDMENT
